coverage: 100% of reachable node types; the browser evaluates all 1,007 nodes in the root closure.
validation: Four Blender/VM cases cover authored settings, wrapper switching, thickness, and wrapper dimensions. The VM now evaluates the authored three-pass sharpen group instead of bypassing it, and Gradient Direction normalizes on the face domain before point interpolation. Authored output improves from 62,109 / 53,890 to 61,659 / 53,893 versus Blender's 61,812 / 53,892 (0.25% low in vertices and one face high), with bounds within 0.0303 units. Thickness 6 is within 0.46% vertices, 19 faces, and 0.0372 units; the 60 by 45 wrapper is within 0.13% vertices, two faces, and 0.0100 units. The first remaining topology divergence is upstream Merge by Distance at 53,907 / 53,893 versus 53,906 / 53,892; the residual split-vertex count follows small Gradient Direction differences around the 30-degree autosmooth threshold.
